pack_purpose: Deterministic narrow follow-up pack for targeted escalation. It defines the crux questions and operational budgets, while source choice may remain model-driven within runtime-enforced tool guardrails.
verification_questions: Does the latest available information materially change the decision, given: BTC can move enough overnight or in the Apr 17 US-morning window to flip a near-threshold one-minute close market.?
How decision-relevant is the remaining contract/source-of-truth ambiguity: minor operational sensitivity around exact Binance noon-ET candle mapping/final display, but rules are otherwise clear?
Are the current blockers still material to final judgment: Thin cushion above strike relative to normal BTC intraday volatility?
Is the main verification gap still decision-material: No independent short-horizon volatility model or fresh catalyst map was available for the final pre-settlement window.?

structured_handoff_focus.contract_ambiguity_reason: minor operational sensitivity around exact Binance noon-ET candle mapping/final display, but rules are otherwise clear
structured_handoff_focus.verification_gap_summary: No independent short-horizon volatility model or fresh catalyst map was available for the final pre-settlement window.
structured_handoff_focus.best_countercase_summary: If BTC simply holds roughly flat from current mid-74k levels, the market’s 65% Yes price may be fair or slightly cheap.
structured_handoff_focus.main_reason_for_disagreement: The main disagreement is how much confidence current above-strike spot deserves in a single-minute settlement market.
structured_handoff_focus.resolution_mechanics_summary: Yes resolves only if the Binance BTC/USDT candle labeled 12:00 ET on Apr 17 has a final Close strictly above 74000.
structured_handoff_focus.freshness_driver: BTC can move enough overnight or in the Apr 17 US-morning window to flip a near-threshold one-minute close market.
decision_blockers: Thin cushion above strike relative to normal BTC intraday volatility
Outcome depends on one exact future Binance 1-minute close rather than broader daily price behavior
No strong independent verification of overnight/morning catalyst risk beyond spot-and-rules checks

supporting_persona_summaries.main_thesis: The market's ~65% Yes price is broadly defensible because Binance BTC/USDT was already modestly above 74,000, but the narrow one-minute noon ET resolution window keeps meaningful reversal risk alive, so fair value looks slightly lower around 61%.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market's ~65% Yes price as an information-rich prior.
Check the governing rules and confirm the exact Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close is the source of truth.
Verify that Binance spot and recent 1-minute closes are currently above 74,000 and that Coinbase is broadly aligned.
Discount somewhat for the narrow timing condition and small cushion above strike.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT spot was about 74,645 at research time, already above the strike.
Recent Binance 1-minute closes fetched during research were all above 74,000.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact future one-minute close, and BTC was only modestly above the strike.
A normal sub-1% downside move by noon ET could flip the outcome to No.
unresolved_ambiguities: How much persistence should be inferred from current-above-strike spot context into the specific noon ET minute.
Whether any material overnight or morning catalyst emerges before settlement.
supporting_persona_summaries.what_would_change_view: I would turn more bearish if Binance traded back below 74,000 into the late-morning ET window or if crypto risk sentiment rolled over sharply; I would turn more bullish if BTC built a larger sustained buffer above 74,000 before settlement.
supporting_persona_summaries.timing_relevance: High timing sensitivity: the contract depends on the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute candle close on April 17, so ordinary overnight or morning volatility remains decision-relevant.
supporting_persona_summaries.source_quality_view: Source quality is solid for a medium-difficulty short-horizon case: Polymarket rules give clear resolution mechanics, Binance API is close to the settlement venue, and Coinbase provides a modest independent contextual cross-check; source-of-truth ambiguity is low.
